seasonality: Event-driven: TVL and fee activity spike around major protocol upgrades (Chang hard fork, Hydra milestones) and DeFi protocol launches on Cardano rather than showing consistent seasonal patterns.
rationale: Cardano's annualized on-chain fee revenue is approximately $8.2M (DefiLlama fees page, trailing 30-day extrapolated), against a fully diluted market cap near ~$33.6B (CoinGecko, ADA ~$0.96 × 35B supply), yielding an extremely high P/S of ~4,100 — reflecting that Cardano remains lightly used relative to its valuation. TVL of ~$447M (DefiLlama, Cardano chain page) gives a P/TVL of ~31x, also elevated versus Ethereum (~1.1x) and Solana (~3x). Real yield to stakers is effectively null because staking rewards (~2.8% APR) are entirely inflationary (reserve emissions), not derived from fee revenue redistribution; protocol fees are burned or go to the treasury, not directly to stakers as 'real yield'.
